summary.oneLine: Tomato–pepper–onion rice cooked in seasoned stock until fluffy and deeply colored.
notes: This is a canonical SSA baseline recipe designed for deterministic planning and adaptation.
Regional variants vary on heat, smoky notes, and sweetness. Sweetness should come from onions/peppers, not added sugar.
Nutrition is intentionally left as an estimate placeholder for SSA nutrition pipeline to compute.
If cooked with stock cubes that contain allergens, ensure household allergen rules are applied.

ingredients.label: Long-grain white rice
notes: Rinse until water runs mostly clear; drain well.

ingredients.label: Tomatoes
notes: Roma/plum preferred for thicker sauce; adjust cookdown if watery.

ingredients.label: Tomato paste
notes: Helps deepen color and reduce watery flavor.

ingredients.label: Yellow onion
notes: One for blending/chopping into base; one for sauté (or both blended).

ingredients.label: Scotch bonnet (or habanero)
notes: Use gloves; start with half for low-medium heat.

ingredients.label: Neutral oil
notes: Can reduce to 1/4 cup for lighter finish.

ingredients.label: Chicken or beef stock
notes: If not using stock, use water and adjust seasoning.

ingredients.label: Water
notes: Total liquid is stock + water; adjust by rice brand and pot seal.

ingredients.label: Salt
notes: Start with 1 tsp if using salted stock or cubes; adjust at end.

ingredients.label: Stock cube / seasoning cube
notes: Optional; ensure household preferences allow and allergen rules apply.

steps.title: Rinse and drain the rice
steps.body: Rinse rice in cool water until water runs mostly clear. Drain very well to avoid gummy texture.

steps.title: Make the tomato-pepper base
steps.body: Blend (or finely chop) tomatoes, bell pepper, 1 onion, garlic, ginger, and hot pepper (optional) until smooth. If very watery, you may simmer the blend uncovered 5–10 minutes to reduce.

hazards.message: Use gloves when handling very hot peppers; avoid touching eyes/face and ventilate when blending.

steps.title: Sauté onion and fry tomato paste
steps.body: Heat oil in a lidded pot over medium heat. Add remaining onion (sliced or chopped) and cook 3–5 minutes until soft. Add tomato paste and stir constantly 2–3 minutes to caramelize slightly (reduces raw taste).

steps.title: Cook down the base
steps.body: Pour in blended base carefully (it may splatter). Add thyme, bay leaves, black pepper, and seasoning cube (optional). Simmer uncovered 12–18 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the base deepens in color and thickens.

hazards.message: Tomato base can splatter; reduce heat and stir carefully.

steps.title: Add stock/water and season
steps.body: Add stock and water. Bring to a gentle boil. Taste the liquid and adjust salt so it tastes slightly saltier than you want the finished rice (rice will absorb seasoning).

steps.title: Add rice and cook covered
steps.body: Stir in drained rice. Reduce heat to low, cover tightly, and cook 20–25 minutes until rice is tender and liquid absorbed. Avoid lifting the lid too often; check once at 20 minutes.

steps.title: Steam and rest, then fluff
steps.body: Turn off heat. Keep covered 8–10 minutes to steam and finish. Remove bay leaves, then fluff gently with a fork. Taste and adjust salt/pepper. If rice is still firm, sprinkle 2–4 tbsp water, cover, and steam 5 more minutes.

suggestions: Serve with grilled chicken, beef, fish, or stewed beans.
Optional sides: fried plantain, simple salad/coleslaw.

reheatNotes: Reheat with a splash of water/stock covered to restore moisture.
Cool rice quickly before refrigerating for food safety.

substitutions.notes: Habanero is closest in fruity heat; start small and adjust.
If using only water, increase seasoning (salt/spices) carefully.

equipmentFallbacks.doInstead: Finely chop tomatoes, peppers, onion, garlic, ginger; simmer longer to break down.

allergenConsiderations.notes: Use stock and careful seasoning for umami instead.

knownGood: Liquid should taste a bit stronger than desired finished rice before adding rice.
Base should be cooked down to reduce raw tomato taste.

commonIssues.likelyCause: Rice not drained well or too much liquid
commonIssues.fix: Drain thoroughly; reduce total liquid slightly next time; keep lid sealed.

commonIssues.likelyCause: Heat too high or pot not heavy enough
commonIssues.fix: Use a heavier pot, lower heat, consider a heat diffuser.

commonIssues.likelyCause: Base not cooked down long enough
commonIssues.fix: Simmer base longer until deeper color and aroma; fry tomato paste well.
